summary: Justin teaches systematic market due diligence by personally visiting comps, researching major employers, tracking population growth trends (targeting 1%+ annually), and studying municipal development websites for future infrastructure projects. He emphasizes that boots-on-the-ground research often uncovers pricing discrepancies with broker OMs and reveals competitive advantages investors will respect.
takeaways: Visit and walk all comparable properties in person—never rely solely on online maps or apartments.com
Broker OMs can be 3+ months outdated; your on-the-ground comp research generates insider information that counters stale data
Target markets with population growth of 1% per year or more
Research major employers and understand where tenant base works and lives
Check city development websites for approved/pending projects: light rail, industrial, retail, airports, parks
Identify 'path of progress' infrastructure like light rail systems coming within 2-3 years—these drive people, jobs, and rents
Know your comps in detail so you can defend your rent projections with data and facts against lender/investor pushback

skill_candidates.change: Create a systematic comp-checking process: visit physically, document rent vs. broker OM discrepancies, and build a personal market database
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'boots on the ground driving comps and getting into those complexes' and describes discovering rents $150 higher than broker OM; he repeats 'you need to know your comps' three times for emphasis

skill_candidates.change: Screen markets for population growth ≥1% annually before pursuing deals
skill_candidates.evidence: 'Typically you want to see about 1% a year or more' population growth; presented as explicit threshold for market qualification

skill_candidates.change: Counter rent projection challenges by citing primary market research (employer base, development sites, infrastructure timelines) rather than broker materials
skill_candidates.evidence: 'you can counter anybody too that wants to say, oh, like, oh, rents aren't here...Really? Let me show you why. Right? Counter it. But with data and facts'

title: Lesson 3: Market Due Diligence
